dataset.name: analytics.customer_orders
dataset.description: Customer order facts used by retention, revenue, and executive reporting.

summary: 3 finding(s) across 3 parsed statement(s); DataHub reports 2 downstream asset(s).

findings.title: Drop Column can break downstream consumers
findings.evidence: Column `email` is targeted by `drop column`. DataHub classifies it with urn:li:tag:PII, urn:li:tag:Sensitive, urn:li:glossaryTerm:PersonalEmail. DataHub lineage shows 1 downstream asset(s): ml.churn_features.
findings.recommendation: Use an expand-and-contract migration: add the replacement, backfill it, dual-write, migrate every downstream consumer, and remove the old column only after DataHub lineage and usage checks are clean.

findings.title: Non-null column is added without a default
findings.evidence: New column `lifecycle_status` is `NOT NULL` without a default.
findings.recommendation: Add the column as nullable, backfill existing rows, validate completeness, then add the non-null constraint in a later deployment.

findings.title: Wildcard projection hides schema coupling
findings.evidence: The SQL contains `SELECT *`, so new or reordered columns can leak downstream.
findings.recommendation: List the required columns explicitly and preserve stable output names.

safe_migration_plan: Create an additive schema change and keep the current contract intact.
Backfill and validate the replacement using explicit row-count and null checks.
Notify DataHub owners and migrate the listed downstream assets.
Run the review again after DataHub lineage and usage metadata refresh.
Remove the old field only after a monitored compatibility window and rollback checkpoint.
